quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Open tube harmonics"?

options.text: Both ends are antinodes: f_n = nv/(2L), n = 1,2,3,...; all harmonics present; e.g., flute, open organ pipe
d_o > 0 (real object); d_i > 0 (real image, opposite side); d_i  0 (converging); f < 0 (diverging)
Different wavelengths refract by different amounts (n varies with λ); produces spectrum separation in prisms and rainbows
High-voltage transmission minimizes I²R losses: P_loss = I²R = P²R/V²; higher V → lower I → much lower losses

quiz_questions.explanation: "Open tube harmonics" is defined as: Both ends are antinodes: f_n = nv/(2L), n = 1,2,3,...; all harmonics present; e.g., flute, open organ pipe. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Closed tube harmonics"?

options.text: The image from lens 1 serves as the object for lens 2; d_o2 = separation - d_i1; apply thin lens equation sequentially
Node at closed end, antinode at open: f_n = nv/(4L), n = 1,3,5,... (odd harmonics only); e.g., clarinet, stopped pipe
f_observed = f_source · (v ± v_observer)/(v ∓ v_source); approaching gives higher pitch, receding gives lower pitch
F_b = ρ_fluid · V_displaced · g; an object floats when its weight equals the buoyant force (average density < fluid density)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Closed tube harmonics" is defined as: Node at closed end, antinode at open: f_n = nv/(4L), n = 1,3,5,... (odd harmonics only); e.g., clarinet, stopped pipe. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Inverse-square law"?

options.text: High-voltage transmission minimizes I²R losses: P_loss = I²R = P²R/V²; higher V → lower I → much lower losses
Strong attraction and permanent magnetization; magnetic domains align cooperatively; responsible for permanent magnets
Zero electrical resistance below a critical temperature T_c; enables powerful electromagnets (MRI: 1.5-7T fields) and lossless power transmission
I = P/(4πr²); doubling the distance reduces intensity by a factor of 4; explains why sound fades rapidly with distance

quiz_questions.explanation: "Inverse-square law" is defined as: I = P/(4πr²); doubling the distance reduces intensity by a factor of 4; explains why sound fades rapidly with distance. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Resonance in tubes"?

options.text: When a driving frequency matches a natural frequency of the tube, standing waves form and the sound is amplified dramatically
B = μ₀I/(2πr); circular field lines around a long straight wire; strength decreases with distance r from wire
Objects underwater appear shallower than they are due to refraction: apparent depth = actual depth × (n₂/n₁)
Total charge in an isolated system remains constant; charges can transfer between objects but cannot be created or destroyed

quiz_questions.explanation: "Resonance in tubes" is defined as: When a driving frequency matches a natural frequency of the tube, standing waves form and the sound is amplified dramatically. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Sound level addition"?

options.text: Bright colored lines on a dark background; produced by excited atoms emitting photons at characteristic frequencies
B = μ₀I/(2πr); circular field lines around a long straight wire; strength decreases with distance r from wire
Two identical sources: I_total = 2I → increase of 10 log₁₀(2) ≈ 3 dB; doubling the number of sources adds ≈3 dB
Total charge in an isolated system remains constant; charges can transfer between objects but cannot be created or destroyed

quiz_questions.explanation: "Sound level addition" is defined as: Two identical sources: I_total = 2I → increase of 10 log₁₀(2) ≈ 3 dB; doubling the number of sources adds ≈3 dB. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Both ends are antinodes: f_n = nv/(2L), n = 1,2,3,...; all harmonics present; e.g., flute, open organ pipe"?

options.text: Magnetic flux
Speed of sound
Resonance
Open tube harmonics

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Open tube harmonics".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Node at closed end, antinode at open: f_n = nv/(4L), n = 1,3,5,... (odd harmonics only); e.g., clarinet, stopped pipe"?

options.text: Closed tube harmonics
Wave interference
Root-mean-square speed
Energy levels

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Closed tube harmonics".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "I = P/(4πr²); doubling the distance reduces intensity by a factor of 4; explains why sound fades rapidly with distance"?

options.text: Speed of sound
Rayleigh criterion
Quantum tunneling
Inverse-square law

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Inverse-square law".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "When a driving frequency matches a natural frequency of the tube, standing waves form and the sound is amplified dramatically"?

options.text: Quantum mechanical model
Chain reaction
Resonance in tubes
Nuclear binding energy

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Resonance in tubes".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Two identical sources: I_total = 2I → increase of 10 log₁₀(2) ≈ 3 dB; doubling the number of sources adds ≈3 dB"?

options.text: Mass spectrometer
Quantum mechanical model
Sound level addition
Heisenberg uncertainty principle

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Sound level addition".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Sound and Acoustics is accurate?

options.text: Sound and Acoustics analyzes sound wave behavior in detail.
Electric Potential relates to the potential energy per unit charge in an electric field.
Diverging lenses always produce virtual, upright, reduced images.
Electric Circuits involve the flow of charge through conductive paths driven by a potential difference.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Sound and Acoustics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: Standing waves in tubes: open tube has antinodes at both ends (harmonics: f_n = nv/2L); closed tube has a node at the closed end and antinode at the open end (odd harmonics only: f_n = nv/4L, n = 1,3,5,...).
This force is short-range (~1 fm), extremely strong, and charge-independent.
Applications of Quantum Ideas extends quantum principles to technologies and fundamental physics.
Mass and energy are interconvertible: the mass defect in nuclear reactions is converted to kinetic energy of products and radiation.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Sound and Acoustics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: Fission: a heavy nucleus (e.g., ²³⁵U) splits into lighter nuclei + neutrons + energy.
Electric Potential relates to the potential energy per unit charge in an electric field.
Sound intensity follows the inverse-square law: I = P/(4πr²).
The Rydberg formula for hydrogen: 1/λ = R_H(1/n_f² - 1/n_i²), where R_H = 1.097 × 10⁷ m⁻¹.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Sound and Acoustics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: The speed of sound depends on the medium's elastic and inertial properties: v = √(B/ρ) for fluids and v ≈ 331 + 0.6T m/s in air (T in °C).
Electric Circuits involve the flow of charge through conductive paths driven by a potential difference.
Thin-film interference involves path length differences in reflected light: constructive or destructive depending on the film thickness, wavelength, and indices of refraction.
The strong nuclear force binds nucleons together, overcoming electrostatic repulsion between protons.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Sound and Acoustics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: Fission: a heavy nucleus (e.g., ²³⁵U) splits into lighter nuclei + neutrons + energy.
Sound and Acoustics analyzes sound wave behavior in detail.
EMF can be induced by changing B, changing A, or changing the angle θ between B and the area vector.
Sound is a longitudinal mechanical wave produced by vibrating objects.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Sound and Acoustics as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: Concert hall acoustics: architects design room dimensions, wall shapes, and absorption materials to optimize reverberation time (1.5-2.5 seconds ideal for orchestras)
Charged particle therapy: proton beams for cancer treatment are steered using electromagnetic forces to precisely target tumors deep in the body
Velocity selectors in mass spectrometers: only ions with the correct speed enter the separation chamber, improving measurement accuracy
Endoscopes use fiber optic bundles and tiny lens systems to allow doctors to view inside the body without surgery—used in colonoscopy, arthroscopy, and laparoscopy

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: PET-CT combines metabolic imaging (PET using ¹⁸F-FDG) with anatomical imaging (CT X-rays) for precise cancer diagnosis and treatment monitoring
Charged particle therapy: proton beams for cancer treatment are steered using electromagnetic forces to precisely target tumors deep in the body
Musical instrument design: the length and shape of brass instruments determine which harmonics are produced; a bugle plays only natural harmonics of its tube length
Velocity selectors in mass spectrometers: only ions with the correct speed enter the separation chamber, improving measurement accuracy

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Sound and Acoustics applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Stellar composition: absorption lines in starlight reveal which elements are in the star's atmosphere; helium was first discovered in the Sun's spectrum before being found on Earth
Camera lens design: modern camera lenses contain 10-20 elements to correct for aberrations, provide zoom capability, and maintain image sharpness across the frame
Ultrasound imaging: frequencies of 2-18 MHz (very short wavelength) provide high-resolution images of internal body structures while being safe (non-ionizing)
Quantum computing uses superposition and entanglement to process information exponentially faster for certain problems (factoring, simulation, optimization)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Sound and Acoustics?

options.text: Concert hall acoustics: architects design room dimensions, wall shapes, and absorption materials to optimize reverberation time (1.5-2.5 seconds ideal for orchestras)
LED color selection: the semiconductor band gap determines the emitted photon wavelength; engineers tune the gap to produce specific colors (red, blue, green, white)
The James Webb Space Telescope uses a 6.5m segmented gold-coated mirror to collect infrared light from the earliest galaxies in the universe
Cameras: converging lenses focus light from objects at varying distances onto the film/sensor by adjusting the lens-to-sensor distance (focusing)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Sound and Acoustics in practice?

options.text: Quantum computing uses superposition and entanglement to process information exponentially faster for certain problems (factoring, simulation, optimization)
Weather balloons expand as they rise because atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, and Boyle's law predicts volume increase at lower pressure
Battery internal resistance: real batteries have internal resistance r, so terminal voltage = EMF - Ir; explains why batteries 'die' under heavy load
Musical instrument design: the length and shape of brass instruments determine which harmonics are produced; a bugle plays only natural harmonics of its tube length

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Sound and Acoustics.
